solutions.solution: Statement: Should brother-sister marriage be allowed? Argument: No. Incest is a sin.
Statement: Should capitalism fee in private professional colleges be abolished? Argument: Yes. Money should not be the criterion in the selection of students, but merit should be.

Statement: Should one be using mosquito-repelling coils or mats? Argument: No. The fumes emanating from these devices are harmful to health. The argument is incorrect because it is an established fact that the fumes emanating from these devices are almost completely harmless to human beings.

Statement: Should the Government hold competitive examinations for all public sector jobs? Argument: Yes. The system of competitive examinations is free from corruption and hence deserving candidates will be selected. This argument says that competitive exams are free from corruption but our experiences tell us that some degree of corruption does exist in competitive exams. Remember the question-paper leakage of UPSC Civil Services Exam in 1997! Hence, this argument can be rejected.
Statement: Should the culprits behind the Bofors scandal be punished? Argument: No. It would create a political vacuum.

Statement: Should brother-sister marriage be allowed? Argument: Yes. If both are mature and willing then they cannot be prohibited from doing it. This argument promotes incest and can, therefore, be rejected.
Statement: Should all sectarian religions be replaced by one common religion? Argument: No. It would lead to chaos.
Statement: Should one follow a more or less rigid programme daily? Argument: No. It will kill creativity in individuals.
Statement: The mind can be sound if the body is sound. Argument: Yes. A sound body always has a sound mind.
Statement: Should all property belong to the state concerned? Argument: No. In that case, people will not be serious to work hard.

Statement: Should the specialists administer their departments and not the generalists? Argument: No. Because the specialists are generally poor administrators. In all the examples above, the argument makes some assertions. These assertions may or may not follow in actual practice and if the author of the argument insists upon these (assertions), it is his individual perception or assumption. For these reasons, these arguments can be rejected as weak arguments. [For example, it might be the author's view that right to property is as important as right to live. One may or may not agree with the assumption.]
